name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: @JRosenworcelFCC @FCC I am looking forward to more chats in the future. It was great to discuss the strides we have made in bridging the #DigitalDivide, closing the #HomeworkGap, and the work that still needs to be done to improve access to education for all. #TelecomActChat

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: @FCC The pandemic highlighted and exacerbated the issues we were already aware of with the #DigitalDivide. Many households do not have access to broadband and that leaves them behind in school, jobs, and everyday life. We need connections now more than ever. #TelecomActChat

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: @JRosenworcelFCC @FCC So much of school today requires the internet. We risk widening the achievement gap if we do not fix this soon. We need to make sure the internet is affordable and accessible for everyone. #TelecomActChat

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: @JRosenworcelFCC @FCC The #homeworkgap needs attention. Millions of kids across this country—in rural and urban and everywhere in between—can not do their homework or participate in class during the #COVID19 pandemic because they cannot get online. #TelecomActChat

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: @JRosenworcelFCC @FCC This is an issue that is close to my heart. It is something that I have seen firsthand - students struggling through lack of internet access in Waterbury, Connecticut. #TelecomActChat

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: @JRosenworcelFCC @FCC But now, we need to expand #ERate beyond schools and into the homes of students as so many kids are learning remotely during this pandemic. #TelecomActChat

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: @JRosenworcelFCC @FCC #ERate and being able to use the internet to expand my students’ horizons was critical. #TelecomActChat

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: @FCC This bill created a Universal Service Fund to support access by those in rural areas, as well as low-income Americans. It demonstrated that broadband connectivity must reach all Americans, regardless of who they are or where they live. #TelecomActChat

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: @FCC The Telecom Act started us down the path of ever-expanding network connectivity. It recognized the importance of digital access and equity – something we are still working on today. And it codified the idea that the internet should be affordable for all.  #TelecomActChat

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: @JRosenworcelFCC @FCC As a career educator, I am especially interested in how this law brought the internet into schools and libraries. How it has changed how teachers teach and students learn. #TelecomActChat

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: @JRosenworcelFCC @FCC Thanks @FCC & Acting Chairwoman @JRosenworcelFCC for chatting today. It is always a privilege to take a break from my day job in Congress and return to what I love best - teaching. #TelecomActChat

tweet: It’s simple: hungry students don’t learn. The Closing the College Hunger Gap Act would mandate that low-income students are notified of their SNAP eligibility when they apply for student aid.

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: I met with students, food pantry directors and college officials this spring, who told me something I was already familiar with from my experience in the classroom: food insecurity isn’t just a hunger problem- it is a threat to college completion.

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: The current stats are shocking:    >2 million at-risk students potentially eligible for SNAP did not receive benefits. >30% of UConn students report skipping meals. >30% of undergrad ECSU students report being food insecure.

name: Jahana Hayes
tweet: Food insecurity and hunger is a problem on CT college campuses, and we have not done enough to address it. That stops now.
